data.title: Coinbase stock falls as it misses out on S&P500 inclusion
data.description:
Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) shares fell 5% in premarket trade as the firm failed to make the cut in the latest S&P500 rebalancing.

DoorDash (DASH), TKO (TKO), Williams-Sonoma (WSM) and Expand Energy (EXE) will join the S&P 500, according to S&P Dow Jones Indices.

Interactive Brokers, Coinbase Global, Ares Management, Robinhood Markets, LPL Financial, Blue Owl Capital, and Tradeweb Markets were among the possible candidates for S&P 500 inclusion, according to a Barclays note, Barron's reported.

Other crypto-linked stocks were also down, and bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell 2.3% to $83.7K. The fall comes amid a broader risk-off sentiment among investors as President Donald Trump failed to rule out the possibility of a recession in an interview with Fox News. 

Applovin (NASDAQ:APP) was also down 6.3% as it did not make it in the latest S&P 500 rebalancing. The firm was also one of the favorites to be included in the list.

data.title: Chinese investors quietly invest millions in Elon Musk's private ventures - report
data.description:
Wealthy Chinese investors are quietly investing millions of dollars in Elon Musk's private companies including SpaceX (SPACE), xAI, and Neuralink, the Financial Times reported after speaking to China-based asset managers. 

The investments are done through special-purpose vehicles to avoid disclosing the investors' identities. Because of this, it's unclear how much Chinese capital has flowed into Musk's private firms.

Three Chinese-backed asset managers told FT that they sold Chinese investors over $30M worth of shares in SpaceX (SPACE), xAI and Neuralink over the past two years. These investments have little to do with technology transfer or influencing policy, and are primarily profit-driven, according to people involved in the deals.

The appeal of Musk's ventures has also grown in the backdrop of Beijing's restrictions on private sector companies. "China is facing an oversupply of capital and a shortage of high-quality projects," a New York investment manager told FT.

While the investments in Musk's companies are legal, they raise concerns about potential conflicts of interest as the billionaire is a senior advisor to U.S. President Donald Trump. China-based asset managers are said to be promoting Musk's ties to Trump to attract investments from rich Chinese investors.

Derek Scissors, a senior fellow at the American Enterprise Institute, claimed the influx of Chinese money into Musk's companies "adds to this picture that he is more interested in his reputation and his brand in China than he is in American interests."

data.title: Biggest stock movers Monday: Crypto stocks, CKPT, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower Monday as investors weighed uncertainty around President Trump’s tariff policy and awaited key economic data releases this week. Meanwhile, Bitcoin slipped following an executive order aimed at establishing a strategic U.S. bitcoin reserve.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Checkpoint Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CKPT) +66% – Shares surged after India’s Sun Pharma announced a $355M acquisition, offering $4.10 per share in cash, a 66% premium to CKPT’s last close. Shareholders will also receive up to $0.70 per share in contingent value rights based on milestone achievements. The deal, expected to close in Q2 2025, strengthens Sun Pharma’s global onco-dermatology portfolio with CKPT’s FDA-approved treatment, UNLOXCYT (cosibelimab-ipdl).
  • DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) +6% – Shares climbed after S&P Dow Jones Indices announced the food delivery giant will join the S&P 500, replacing BorgWarner (NYSE:BWA) before trading begins on March 24.

Biggest stock losers

  • Bitcoin dropped to the $81K level, pressuring crypto stocks— Bitcoin extended its decline, falling 5.3% to $81,286, marking its fifth straight session in the red after President Trump’s Bitcoin reserve plan failed to boost sentiment. Crypto-related equities took a hit, with MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) -7.2%, Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) -6.4%, Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) -5.1%, MARA Holdings (NASDAQ:MARA) -6.7%, Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) -4.3%, CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK) -5.2%, Hut 8 (NASDAQ:HUT) -5.6%, and Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) -6.5% trading lower.

data.title: European markets edge lower as trade, geopolitical uncertainty remain in focus
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.25%

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.29% 

Germany records trade surplus of €16 billion in January.

France (CAC:IND) -0.06%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) ticked 0.11% lower on Monday amid lingering uncertainties over U.S. tariffs, while investors also braced for more geopolitical action this week at a meeting between U.S. officials and their Ukrainian counterparts.

Furthermore, adding to the bearish sentiment, China’s return to deflation has fuelled worries about global growth prospects.

Coming up in the session: Light calendar. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 7 basis points to 4.25%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points at 2.80%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points at 4.62%.

data.title: Marqeta, Spendesk partner for SMBs expense management across Europe
data.description:
Marqeta (NASDAQ:MQ) has partnered with Spendesk Financial Services as their issuer processor in Europe.

This collaboration enhances Spendesk's spend management and procurement platform, serving over 4,000 SMBs across Europe, by implementing dynamic controls for real-time expense screening and authorization.

Spendesk Financial Services integrated Marqeta's card processing into its Core Banking Platform using Marqeta's flexible platform, offering customers tailored physical and virtual cards.

The partnership enables businesses to implement time-specific spending restrictions, monitor unusual transactions, and approve/deny transactions in real-time, significantly reducing friction in expense management processes.

data.title: Diversified Energy, FuelCell Energy and TESIAC form strategic partnership
data.description:
Diversified Energy (OTCQX:DEC), FuelCell Energy (NASDAQ:FCEL) and TESIAC to supply as much as 360 megawatts of electricity to three distinct locations in Virginia, West Virginia, and Kentucky, the companies said on Monday.

The partnership has agreed to create an acquisition and development company focused on delivering reliable, cost-efficient, net-zero power from natural gas and captured coal mine methane to meet the soaring demand of data centres for reliable power.

Diversified Energy would supply natural gas and CMM or captured waste methane from coal mines that otherwise would have been vented into the atmosphere, from its Appalachian Basin production as the base fuel.

Projects aim to be responsive to the energy needs of data centers by offering an abundant supply of operational power within two years.

data.title: Wolters Kluwer appoints Lisa Nelson as CEO of Financial & Corporate Compliance division
XPeng eyes mass production of flying cars, humanoid robots by 2026
data.description:
XPeng (NYSE:XPEV) is planning to mass-produce flying cars by 2026, making it the first company in the world to do so, according to its CEO He Xiaopeng, state-owned newspaper China Daily reported.

His statements initially lifted the electric vehicle maker's Hong Kong-listed shares around 7% on Monday. But the shares later reversed gains to trade 1% lower amid broader declines in the region's stock market.

"We are integrating vehicle control with AI to develop a new flight control system into such flying cars, making it safer and more user-friendly," He said in a group interview on the sidelines of the annual meeting of China's parliament over the weekend.

He said XPeng's (NYSE:XPEV) Level 3 (L3) autonomous driving technology will surpass global competitors across the board this year. "By 2026, XPeng aims to explore L4 driverless capabilities in parking scenarios, including fully driverless parking and vehicle retrieval in parking lots."

The executive added that , XPeng (NYSE:XPEV) is targeting mass production of industrial humanoid robots with L3 capabilities by next year, "which involves fully integrating their hands, feet, mouth, eyes, and brain."

data.title: ServiceNow nears largest acquisition yet with $3B Moveworks deal - report
data.description:
ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) is nearing a deal to acquire Mountain View, California-based artificial intelligence firm Moveworks, which, if finalized, would be its biggest deal to date, Bloomberg reported citing people familiar with the matter.

The deal could be announced as soon as the coming days, the people said, and could value Moveworks at close to $3B. Although discussions are at an advanced stage, they could still face delays or fall through, the people added, who requested anonymity due to the sensitivity of the information.

Created in 2016, Moveworks offers an agentic AI Assistant that automates support across the entire enterprise operations. Its solution is used by 350+ large enterprises, including Palo Alto Networks, Siemens, Toyota, and Unilever.

The business surpassed $100M annual recurring revenue in September last year and has raised $315M in funding at a $2.1B valuation from investors such as Alkeon, Bain, Iconiq, Kleiner Perkins, Lightspeed, Sapphire, and Tiger Global.

Under the leadership of CEO Bill McDermott, ServiceNow has been integrating generative AI capabilities into its offerings. Before joining ServiceNow, McDermott served as the CEO of software giant SAP SE, where he spearheaded several major acquisitions.

"AI is fueling a top to bottom re-ordering of the enterprise technology landscape," McDermott said during the software firm’s latest earnings release.

Shares of ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) have fallen nearly 20% year-to-date.

data.title: Germany records trade surplus of €16 billion in January, below expectations of €21 billion
data.description:
• Germany's trade surplus fell to €16 billion in January from €20.7 billion in December, below forecasts of €21 billion, marking the smallest trade surplus since last October due to a fall in exports. 

  • Exports declined by 2.5% month-on-month to €129.2 billion in January, meanwhile, imports grew by 1.2% to a 16-month high of €113.1 billion.

  • Industrial production in Germany, meanwhile, increased 2% month-over-month in January 2025, rebounding from a 1.5% decline in December 2024 and beating market expectations of a 1.5% increase. 

 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)  

Currency: (EUR:USD)
